what_it_does: This file specifies the design, schema, and operational plan for a ledger that catalogs code and design substrates in the Company using a two-layer extraction model (deterministic harness + interpretive Opus pass) stored in a local Supabase/Postgres database with pgvector embeddings.
observations: It mandates path-anchored, complete coverage where missing ledger entries are provable by diffing against the directory tree.
It separates facts (extracted deterministically by AST/parsers) from queries/interpretations (derived later over the assembled graph).
It defines three core tables: entry, symbol, and edge, all stamped with provenance.
It explicitly forbids agents from guessing in structured fields or injecting bias via checklists.
It includes self-extending schema mechanisms via extra, suggested_fields, and proposed_edge_kinds.
It documents supersession of earlier parallel experiments and records tracked follow-ups.
It provides concrete setup commands and a systemd timer for scheduled refresh.
standouts: The two-layer model reserves the model for interpretive work only, using deterministic parsing for structural facts.
Completeness is defined as a tree diff, not a claim.
The ledger is intended to be both the inventory and the first working instance of the architecture it describes.
conventions: Follows a structured markdown format with numbered sections and clear hierarchical headings.
Uses precise, intentional vocabulary: laws, no-partial, discovery-not-verification.
Includes explicit status, scope, and supersession notes to prevent drift.
Breaks the convention of flat prose documentation by requiring structured, queryable substrate.
concerns: The scope decision to exclude markdown/prose files is marked as to settle, not silently decided.
Several integrations are explicitly staged but not built this pass, creating follow-up risk if not tracked.
The deterministic harness must support many file types; the spec assumes parsers exist but does not detail them here.
notes: This is the build spec for a structured, queryable ledger that turns the Company's fragmented code/design corpus into a path-anchored graph with deterministic and interpretive layers.
questions: What exactly constitutes the relevant set for Layer B, and how is the triage step implemented in code?
Which specific parser implementations are used for each file type in the deterministic harness?
How are embeddings generated and what text is fed into the pgvector columns?
What is the exact resolution algorithm for turning raw edge to strings into node ids?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be a reusable spec for building a trustworthy ledger; the file does exactly that by specifying schema, process, setup, and supersession, so purpose and actual align.
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish a rigorous, automated way to know what the Company has actually built before any unification work begins, so that future agents and Tim can direct by recognition rather than memory.
novelty: It treats documentation as a queryable graph substrate rather than prose, and explicitly designs the ledger to be a small working instance of the architecture it is meant to serve.
contribution: This file contributes a complete design specification for a path-anchored, deterministic-plus-interpretive ledger with schema, provenance, operational commands, and explicit supersession/follow-up tracking.
summary_for_embedding: A design specification for a path-anchored ledger that catalogs code and design substrates via deterministic parsing and interpretive model passes into Supabase/Postgres tables with graph edges and pgvector embeddings.
intention_for_embedding: To create a trustworthy, queryable inventory of the Company's built artifacts so unification and future development can proceed from grounded, complete knowledge rather than partial memory.
